channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Escalation in Middle East Conflict Following Assassinations of Iranian Officials
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have sharply escalated following the targeted killing of Iranian Intelligence Minister Esmail Khatib by Israeli forces, confirmed by Iranian state media. This follows previ...

item.title: Iranian women’s football team complete return to Iran after asylum withdrawals
item.description: The Iranian women’s football team has returned to Iran via the Turkish border after a complex journey from Australia. Initially, six players and one staff member had sought asylum in Australia, fearin...

item.title: Senegal demands independent investigation after AFCON final ruling favoring Morocco
item.description: Senegal don sharply criticize Confederation of African Football (CAF) after dem award 2025 Africa Cup of Nations (AFCON) title to Morocco, calling the decision 'grossly illegal.' According to Senegal ...

item.title: Federal High Court convicts 10 Filipino sailors and their vessel over cocaine importation in Lagos
item.description: The National Drug Law Enforcement Agency (NDLEA) don secure conviction against ten Filipino sailors plus their vessel, MV Nord Bosporus, for importing 20 kilogrammes of cocaine into Nigeria. The Feder...

item.title: Pakistan’s FM Ishaq Dar in Riyadh to discuss regional peace amid Middle East tensions
item.description: Deputy Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Ishaq Dar has reached Riyadh on Wednesday for a consultative meeting with foreign ministers of Arab and Islamic countries. The focus of the meeting, hosted b...

item.title: Two Teenagers Missing After Car Accident in Wisbech St Mary
item.description: A major search operation is underway in Wisbech St Mary, Cambridgeshire, following a serious road accident in which a car carrying five teenagers plunged into a river. The incident occurred at approxi...

item.title: CCBRT Hospital reduces free medical services as donor support declines
item.description: CCBRT Hospital in Msasani, Dar es Salaam, known for supporting people with disabilities and maternal health, is now reducing some free services due to financial difficulties. Services affected include...

item.title: D’Tigress aims to defeat Philippines in FIBA Women’s World Cup qualifiers
item.description: Nigeria women basketball team, D’Tigress, dey set their mind to win against Philippines dis Saturday for their third match during the FIBA Women’s World Cup qualifiers wey dey Lyon, France. Coach Rena...

item.title: Edison executives receive higher pay despite company’s role in deadly Eaton fire
item.description: Despite Southern California Edison's suspected role in the deadly Eaton fire that killed 19 people and destroyed thousands of homes in Altadena on January 7, 2025, the utility's top executives saw sub...

item.title: India Steps Up Efforts to End Maoist Insurgency Ahead of March 31 Deadline
item.description: The Indian government is intensifying its efforts to eradicate Maoist insurgency as the March 31 deadline approaches. Security forces have increased operations in affected regions, particularly in Chh...

item.title: Differing Priorities Complicate the US-Israel Approach to the Iran Conflict
item.description: Donald Trump and Benjamin Netanyahu have both played significant roles in the current military confrontation with Iran, yet their differing motivations and leadership styles make concluding the confli...

item.title: Edmonton Police Chief Defends Educational Visit to Israel Amid Community Concerns
item.description: Edmonton Police Service Chief Warren Driechel has defended his recent trip to Israel, which he undertook in February with the Major Cities Chiefs Association, a professional organisation of police lea...

item.title: Red Bull’s GamePop Magazine Features a Playable Tetris Cover
item.description: Red Bull’s GamePop magazine has transformed the traditional magazine cover into an interactive gaming experience, creating what is being called the world’s first playable magazine cover. Designed in c...

item.title: Karnataka High Court grants bail to West Bengal woman with minor children after demolition drive incident
item.description: The Karnataka High Court has granted bail to Sarbanu Khatun, a 25-year-old woman from West Bengal, who was arrested in January during a demolition drive in Bengaluru for shouting the slogan 'Jai Bangl...

item.title: 18-day-old baby dies after falling from third-floor flat in London; mother charged with murder
item.description: An 18-day-old infant, Baby Mariam, tragically died after allegedly being thrown from a third-floor flat in Westminster, central London. The incident reportedly occurred while her father was in the kit...

item.title: Pakistan maintains airspace closure for Indian aircraft until April 24, 2026
item.description: Pakistan has decided to continue its restriction on Indian airlines and India-registered aircraft from using its airspace, with the ban now extended until April 24, 2026. The Pakistan Airports Authori...

item.title: Imran Khan's health assessed by specialist medical board at Adiala jail
item.description: The health of former Prime Minister Imran Khan has been thoroughly assessed by a newly formed medical board at Adiala Jail, Islamabad, following directions from the Islamabad High Court (IHC). The boa...

item.title: Moderate Coffee and Tea Intake Linked to Lower Dementia Risk Over 43 Years
item.description: A long-term study conducted by Mass General Brigham, Harvard T.H. Chan School of Public Health, and the Broad Institute of MIT and Harvard suggests that moderate consumption of caffeinated coffee and ...

item.title: Study finds couples often disturb each other's sleep, suggesting separate bedrooms can improve rest
item.description: A recent study has revealed that couples who share a bed disturb each other's sleep on average six times per night. These disturbances, caused by movements such as rolling over, tugging at covers, or ...

item.title: Sean Penn receives symbolic award from Ukrainian railway carriage
item.description: Actor Sean Penn, 65, was presented with a symbolic 'Oscar' made from the metal of a Ukrainian railway carriage destroyed by a Russian missile. This gesture followed his absence from the official Acade...
